input.initialPrompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project so they can iterate faster on failing tests. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

data.content: I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run your tests whenever you save a file change.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET test project with MSTest. They want hot reload to iterate faster. In .NET, `dotnet watch test` provides hot reload for tests - it watches for file changes and re-runs tests automatically.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` for them in async mode so they can see the output and iterate.

arguments.description: Run dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-4ad9b8d669d042d5ae5c8813f45885ec && dotnet watch test --no-restore 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.content: The watcher started but the test output scrolled by. Let me check the initial test results:
data.reasoningText:
It seems like the initial test run completed but the output was minimal. Let me scroll up to see the full test results. Actually, let me read more output - the test results should be in there. It looks like the watcher ran the tests and exited, and is now waiting for file changes. Let me re-read to get more context.

Actually, looking at the output more carefully:
- `dotnet watch ⌚ Waiting for changes` - it started watching
- `dotnet watch ⌚ Exited` - the test run exited
- `dotnet watch ⏳ Waiting for a file to change before restarting ...` - waiting for changes


The test output likely scrolled past, but the watcher is running and should re-execute tests when files change. The second test should fail since `CalculateShipping("express", 1.0)` returns `10.00m` instead of the expected `15.00m`.

Let me run the tests normally first to see the actual failures before starting the watcher.

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-4ad9b8d669d042d5ae5c8813f45885ec && dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -20", "description": "Run tests to see failures", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content: There's a build error — missing `using System;`. Let me fix that so the project compiles, then the watcher (already running) will pick up the change.
data.reasoningText:
The build is failing because `ArgumentException` needs `using System;`. Also, the express test expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m. Let me not fix the test logic (the user wants to iterate on that), but I should fix the missing `using` so the project at least builds. Actually, wait - the user said they have failing tests and want hot reload. Let me just fix the build error and let the watcher run. They can iterate on the test logic themselves.

Actually, let me re-read - the user said "I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster." So they want the watch setup so they can fix things themselves. Let me:


1. Fix the build error (missing `using System;`)
2. Make sure `dotnet watch test` is running

The watcher is already running in the background, so I'll add the missing using statement and it should pick up the change automatically.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is running and detected your file change. Here's the current status:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expected `15.00` but got `10.00`

The watcher is live in shell `watch`. Every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, it will automatically rebuild and re-run the tests. The fix for the failing test is in the `CalculateShipping` method — the `"express"` case returns `10.00m` instead of `15.00m`.
